mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening aggregate demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or event-study alpha is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and real personal spending confirms robust domestic demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings growth.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side strength and inventory restocking, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no directional bias (win rate < 52%) when INDPRO momentum is positive, or autocorrelation in the signal exceeds 0.8 indicating non-stationarity.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of restrictive monetary policy below 1.0% and unemployment below 5.0% creates a goldilocks environment where cheap capital meets strong labor demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint condition triggers negative 20-day returns in >60% of historical instances, or the interaction effect reverses sign when inflation exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp quarterly decline in housing starts signals collapsing residential investment and broader consumer confidence deterioration, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >58% of triggered quarters, or the housing signal fails to predict earnings revisions over a 3-month horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains household income and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year window, or positive payroll growth coincides with negative equity returns in >65% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month acceleration in the consumer price index triggers immediate expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >62% of triggered months, or the inflation signal shows zero predictive power for short-term equity volatility and direction.
